name: AI Vegetables POV
description: Surrealist POV short: an anthropomorphic vegetable performs a mundane human action (running for the bus, sitting in a meeting, waiting in line). 12-18s TikTok format, 9:16. Hook lands in the first 2s through visual shock. Photorealistic vegetable rendering (not cartoon) is the load-bearing aesthetic choice. Cheap, fast, evergreen viral pattern that works in any language because the gag is visual.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter (multi-ref when the same vegetable must stay consistent across scenes)
stackSummary.i2v: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro via OpenRouter, 5s clips, generate_audio: false
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — short POV line per scene (4-7 words). Language follows the user's brief; works in any language.
stackSummary.music: ElevenLabs Music — quirky upbeat instrumental, force_instrumental: true
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 + HormoziCaptions or TikTokCaptions (large pop)
stackSummary.composition: Remotion 4.0.441 — TransitionSeries fade 6 frames between scenes, music ducked under VO

constants.characterStyle: anthropomorphic vegetable, photorealistic, slight humanoid proportions (limbs sprout organically — not cartoon attachments)
constants.hookWindow: first 2 seconds — visual shock cold open

variationAxes: vegetable type (cucumber, tomato, carrot, potato, eggplant, pepper, onion, broccoli)
setting (subway, kitchen, office, gym, dating, traffic, queue, hospital)
human action being performed (running, working, exercising, dating, waiting)
emotional tone (deadpan, frustrated, ecstatic, melancholic, panicked)
VO language (English by default; Russian deadpan also works strongly for this format)
doNotCopyLiterally: Scenario, VO lines, exact prompts, per-clip durations. This is a vibe reference; the scenarist writes a fresh scenario every project.
referenceNotes: No user reference required. Vegetable + setting are generated from prompts. If the user wants a specific brand or person to appear, escalate to the reference-required gate (see AGENTS.md hard rule #3).
